summary: Excellent 1971 audience recording capturing a psychedelic, top‑notch first set with standout Bertha and The Other One.
review: The tape delivers crystal‑clear highs and a solid frequency range that lets every instrument shine, despite a few missing tracks. Musically the show is a firecracker – a smashing first set highlighted by a monster Bertha, Mr. Charlie, Casey Jones and an inspired Cryptical > Drums > Other One sequence, while the second set runs a bit weaker but remains enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and audio fidelity across many reviews; minor issues (missing songs, possible source swap) do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp highs, solid frequency range and clear instrument separation; a few reviewers note the second‑set may have been sourced differently, but overall the AUD tape is among the best 1971 audience recordings.

standout_songs: Cryptical Envelopment > Drums > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set shines with tight playing and memorable jams; second set is slightly weaker yet still solid, though some listeners suspect a different source for part of it.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgot to sing until the third verse of Bertha but delivers psychedelic guitar work throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s (Bob's) on‑stage encouragement is captured and adds to the tape’s charm.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment in Cryptical > Drums > Other One is highlighted as a standout moment.
